channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hamas says American-Israeli hostage will only be freed if ceasefire is implemented
item.description: Hamas said Saturday that it will only release Israeli-American hostage Edan Alexander if Israel implements the existing ceasefire agreement in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says ISIS chief killed in Iraq
item.description: President Trump on Friday announced that ISIS’s second in command was killed in Iraq this week, touting it as an example of his administration’s “peace through strength” military philosophy.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: Serbia's capital braces for a major anti-government rally as tensions mount
item.description: Huge crowds are converging on downtown Belgrade for a major rally against populist President Aleksandar Vucic and his government.
item.dc:creator: Jovana Gec And Dusan Stojanovic, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why Trump is singling out South Africa and accusing it of being anti-white and anti-American
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — The Trump administration’s decision to expel the South African ambassador is its latest move against a country it has singled out for sanctions and accused of being anti-white and anti-American. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope enters fifth week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia on positive trajectory
item.description: Pope Francis has entered the fifth week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia with signs of recovery continuing on a positive trajectory.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Barry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia and Ukraine launch aerial attacks amid proposed ceasefire talks
item.description: Russia and Ukraine traded heavy aerial blows overnight Saturday, with both sides reporting more than 100 enemy drones over their respective territories.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Killing site in Jalisco, Mexico just the latest in a long series of gruesome discoveries
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Prosecutors in western Mexico confirmed this week the discovery of hundreds of clothing items and bone fragments by a group of people searching for relatives at a previously known cartel training site, exposing major shortcomings in the original investigation.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. military intervention versus cartels "impossible," says Mexico's president
item.description: "He said everything’s on the table, well no, not everything is on the table, nor on the chair, nor on the floor, nor anywhere, not that.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, WGN 9

item.title: US envoy is taking Putin's comments on Ukraine ceasefire proposal to Trump, Kremlin official says
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff to discuss details of the American proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in the war with Ukraine, asking him to convey Moscow's thoughts to Washington, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Thai officials after Uyghur men are deported to China
item.description: The U.S. State Department has announced sanctions on an unknown number of current and former Thai officials for their role in deporting at least 40 Uyghur men to China despite worries that they could be jailed or even face death there.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Philippine ex-President Duterte appears by video link at International Criminal Court hearing
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — Jailed former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte appeared Friday by videoconference before judges at the International Criminal Court, days after his arrest in Manila on murder charges linked to the deadly “ war on drugs ” that he oversaw while in office.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP promotes use of 'Taiwan' over 'Chinese Taipei' in slight to Beijing
item.description: A group of House Republicans is introducing legislation to assert Taiwan as the official name of the independently-governed island in U.S. communication, pushing back against claims to the territory by the Chinese government in Beijing. Rep. Byron Donalds (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Israel faces new Syria challenge as it adjusts to new strategy amid regional power struggle for influence
item.description: Israel is rethinking its Syria strategy following the country's Islamist takeover in December, rising attacks on minorities and growing regional power struggles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's Irish golf course damaged by pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: The incident comes less than a week after pro-Palestinian protestors dug up some of the Trump Turnberry greens in Scotland.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Global alert issued for missing college student Sudiksha Konanki
item.description: Sudiksha Konanki is one of six female University of Pittsburgh students who traveled together to the Dominican Republic for spring break.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: American draws Australian PM's ire after snatching wombat
item.description: Australian immigration officials could bar an American influencer from ever returning Down Under for carrying a baby wombat away from its mother.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas says it will release American hostage, agrees to restart Gaza ceasefire talks
item.description: Hamas said Friday that it agreed to release American Israeli hostage Edan Alexander and the bodies of four other Americans, following a proposal from mediators to resume negotiations with Israel over a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: UN World Food Program to cut food aid for over 1 million people in Myanmar
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The United Nations food agency said on Friday that more than 1 million people in the war-torn nation of Myanmar will be cut off from food assistance due to critical funding shortfalls.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ontario Premier Ford hails 'positive' meeting with Lutnick
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford lauded his Thursday meeting with Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick as “positive” and “productive” after their public rift over tariffs on imported goods.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Poll: Majority of Americans think Trump is too close to Russia
item.description: A majority of Americans think President Trump is too closely aligned with Russia, including more than a quarter of Republicans, a poll released Thursday found.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Russia presents US with demands that need to be met before ending Ukraine war: report
item.description: Russia has reportedly laid out its demands for ending the war in Ukraine and they mirror previous requests made to Kyiv, the U.S. and NATO.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan says insurgents who attacked a passenger train killed 21 hostages while all others freed
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Insurgents who attacked a passenger train carrying 440 passengers in restive southwestern Pakistan killed 21 hostages before security forces killed all 33 of the assailants, and all other passengers were rescued, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Canada suspends 25% electricity tariffs on US
item.description: This comes after what Ford said was a "productive" conversation with U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Evangelical leader says US must protect Syrian Christians from attacks by jihadi terrorists
item.description: A senior evangelical leader has called on the U.S. to not recognize new Syrian regime following bloodshed of religious minorities, including Christians and Alawites, carried out by radical Islamists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British tourist detained in ICE center after visa suspension: Family
item.description: In a statement to NewsNation, an ICE spokesperson said Burke was detained by the agency "for violation of the terms and conditions of her admission.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: US, Ukraine talk peace as Kyiv, Moscow trade drone attacks
item.description: Officials from Ukraine and the United States have gathered in Saudi Arabia to discuss a potential limited ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump labels ex-Australian PM Malcolm Turnbull 'weak' amid spat
item.description: The president's attack on Turnbull came after comments Turnbull made to Bloomberg that Donald Trump's style is an "advantage" to China.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Pope follows Vatican's spiritual retreat from afar as recovery continues
item.description: Pasolini delivered a meditation on “The hope of eternal life,” a theme that was chosen well before Francis was admitted to Rome's Gemelli hospital on Feb. 14 with a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Syria's worst violence in months reopens wounds of the civil war
item.description: An ambush on a Syrian security patrol by gunmen loyal to ousted leader Bashar Assad escalated into clashes that a war monitor estimates have killed more than 1,000 people over four days.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada braces for new leader: Here are Trudeau’s potential successors
item.description: Canada is bracing for Prime Minister Justin Trudeau’s successor, who will follow the Liberal Party leader that has led U.S.’s neighbor to the north since the Obama administration, The Associated Press reported. The change in leadership will come amid frosty U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill more than 20 as Polish prime minister warns against appeasement
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched heavy aerial attacks on Ukraine for a second night Saturday after the United States stopped sharing satellite images with the Ukrainian government, officials said. At least 22 people have been killed. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
